name: pm-orchestrator
description: Always-on PM orchestrator. A blind, stateless coordinator that runs ANY task through a pluggable phase-recipe. Per tick it re-reads the run-state ledger, checks the next phase's gate, dispatches ONE worker (Agent or Workflow, always launched from the main loop -- never fans-out into a worker that re-fans-out), records the worker's compact return blob + artifact path in the ledger, and decides done. It never reads worker output md / source / rules; it authors the ledger ONLY from compact blobs, and transfers memory to workers BY REFERENCE via a context-manifest. Gate-by-risk: auto-dispatch when risk_tier in {T0,T1} and the phase is read-only or reversible-worktree-bounded; HARD owner-confirm only when risk_tier in {T2,T3} and risk_class in {clinical,billing,permission,migration,irreversible-data}, or the phase invokes an owner-gated workflow. Stop enum {DONE, CAP_HIT, BLOCKED, PARKED}.
implementation_kind: agent-orchestrated-pm (blind stateless coordinator over a pluggable phase-recipe; gate-by-risk dispatch; ledger + context-manifest)
required_inputs: task: one-line scope of the run (what to drive to done)
recipe: a preset name (review | espresso | bugfix | feature) OR a custom ordered phase list
review/scope base (branch/sha) for any review/scope-freeze phase
active worktree slug + slot when the recipe contains any phase that edits source (worktrees/<slug>/{fe,be} ONLY)
tier_policy: (model,effort) per phase derived from risk_tier via scripts/tier_policy.json -- passed at dispatch, NEVER baked into the workflow
risk_tier + risk_class per phase (drives the gate-by-risk dispatch rule) -- supplied by scripts/harness_router.py / rule_card
validators: python scripts/worktree.py list       # confirm fix worktree exists before any source-editing phase
python scripts/audit_path.py <base>   # review md path discipline (delegated to deep-review when the recipe includes review)
python scripts/harness_doctor.py      # check_registry must stay green (no dangling manifest refs)

recipes._note: Phase-recipe presets. Each phase = {name, dispatch (entry_skill|workflow), tier (from scripts/tier_policy.json keyed by risk_tier), gate (auto|owner-confirm, COMPUTED by gate_by_risk below), done_check}. The coordinator dispatches each phase from the MAIN LOOP. 'promote' is NEVER a dispatchable phase. Dispatch 'mh-implement', NEVER 'incremental-impl'.

review.summary: Read-only review. Freeze scope, run deep-review, scribe writes findings md.

phases.name: scope-freeze
phases.done_check: frozen file-set recorded in ledger

phases.name: review
phases.done_check: findings md written; counts blob returned

phases.name: scribe-findings
phases.done_check: docs/audit/<date>/<base>.md exists per findings-schema

espresso.summary: The existing review->fix->re-review convergence loop, expressed as a recipe. CANONICAL loop lives in engine/workflows/espresso-test/ -- this preset REUSES it, it does NOT reimplement or modify espresso's files.

phases.name: pre-sweep
phases.done_check: round-0 question batch answered -> decision_preseed

phases.name: loop
phases.done_check: open_block_high == 0 OR round > round_cap

bugfix.summary: RCA -> fix -> review for a single bug. mh-rca (read-only RCA) -> mh-fix (worktree-bounded fix) -> deep-review (verify no regression).

phases.name: rca
phases.done_check: RCA verdict + fix-plan returned

phases.name: fix
phases.done_check: fix landed in worktree; scoped validation passed

phases.name: review
phases.done_check: 0 BLOCK/HIGH open on the fix diff

feature.summary: Token-efficient module build from a BA doc: read the BA doc ONCE (distill) -> design (gated) -> slice (gated) -> fan-out implement PER SLICE by-reference at each slice's own (model,effort)/gate -> converge via the espresso loop. REUSES the SDD skills (ui-spec/technical-design/task-slicing) + mh-implementer + espresso-test VERBATIM -- it does NOT reimplement them. READ-BA-ONCE: the BA doc is read ONCE in 'distill' and is NEVER re-fed to any later worker; later phases ride only on the distilled/owner-authored specs (02-requirements -> 03-ui/08-api -> 10-plan). distill/spec/slice are HARD-GATED (owner-confirm); implement is computed gate-by-risk PER SLICE. Dispatch mh-implementer per slice from the MAIN LOOP (depth-1) -- NEVER mh-implement (re-fans-out -> depth-2, unsupported) NOR incremental-impl.

phases.name: distill
phases.done_check: specs/<m>/02-requirements.md written + a per-slice risk index (risk_tier+risk_class per prospective slice) emitted
phases.note: NEW phase -- fills a real GAP: no SDD skill distills the BA doc today (02-requirements was owner-authored). ONE general read+write worker reads the BA doc (e.g. specs/Tài liệu Nội trú.md) EXACTLY ONCE -> emits 02-requirements + the per-slice risk index. The BA doc path is placed in THIS worker's context-manifest only and is NEVER added to any later worker's manifest. Hard owner-confirm gate (the requirements baseline anchors every downstream phase).

phases.name: spec
phases.done_check: specs/<m>/03-ui.md and/or 08-api.md drafted + owner-approved
phases.note: From distilled 02-requirements + owner-authored 07-schema only (NOT the BA doc). ui-spec -> 03-ui.md (UI reuse map); technical-design -> 08-api.md (API contract). Owner-gated workflows -> always owner-confirm per gate_by_risk.

phases.name: slice
phases.done_check: specs/<m>/10-plan.md written; EACH task carries normalized risk_tier(T0-T3)+risk_class(clinical|billing|permission|migration|other) ALONGSIDE the freeform risk (per engine/skills/task-slicing/DETAILS.md)
phases.note: From the design specs only (NOT the BA doc). Normalized risk_tier+risk_class per task is what lets the implement phase key tier_policy deterministically PER SLICE. Owner-gated -> owner-confirm.

phases.name: implement
phases.fan_out: ONE mh-implementer agent PER DISJOINT SLICE, depth-1 from the MAIN LOOP, ordered by 10-plan dependency rounds
phases.tier: PER-SLICE = scripts/tier_policy.json -> tiers[slice.risk_tier], with risk_class_floor applied when slice.risk_class in {clinical,billing,permission,migration}
phases.done_check: every slice implemented in its worktree; per-slice validation passed; 0 unresolved blockers
phases.note: The PM IS the orchestrator here: it fans out mh-implementer agents DIRECTLY from the main loop (depth-1). Each worker gets ONLY its slice BY REFERENCE per engine/rules/context-manifest.md via engine/prompts/subagent-prompt-template.md: specs/<m>/10-plan.md#task-NN + the specific 03-ui/08-api rows that task touches + rule_card(fe|be) -- NEVER the BA doc, NEVER the whole spec. Per-slice (model,effort) is keyed off that slice's risk_tier (+risk_class_floor). MUST NOT dispatch mh-implement (it re-fans-out -> depth-2, unsupported per README execution note) NOR incremental-impl.

phases.name: converge
phases.done_check: open_block_high == 0 OR round > round_cap
phases.note: Replaces the old one-shot deep-review final phase with the espresso review->fix->re-review convergence loop, REUSED VERBATIM from recipes.espresso (engine/workflows/espresso-test/). The PM does NOT reimplement or modify any espresso file; it dispatches the espresso workflow as this one phase.

single-skill.summary: T1 small scope: ONE skill makes the change + does its OWN diff self-review. No separate review pass. Maps to router recipe 'single-skill'.

phases.name: implement-or-fix
phases.done_check: change landed in worktree; scoped validation passed
phases.note: mh-implement for a small feature/extension; mh-fix for a single known bug. Dispatch the skill, NEVER incremental-impl.

phases.name: self-review
phases.done_check: the skill's built-in diff self-review attached; 0 self-flagged BLOCK/HIGH open
phases.note: REUSES the skill's own self-review (mh-implement/mh-fix self-review their own diff so review converges fast) -- this is NOT a separate deep-review phase.

implement+review.summary: T2 shared/API/DTO/permission/cross-file: implement the change, then a SEPARATE deep-review pass to catch regressions. Maps to router recipe 'implement+review'.

phases.name: implement
phases.done_check: change landed in worktree; scoped validation passed
phases.note: Dispatch mh-implement, NEVER incremental-impl. Single bounded scope -> the mh-implement skill runs directly from the main loop.

phases.name: review
phases.done_check: 0 BLOCK/HIGH open on the change diff

triage-only.summary: Verify a finding/bug list is REAL vs false-positive WITHOUT fixing. mh-rca confirms root cause + mh-reviewer re-checks each item's live evidence -> verdict report. Zero source edits. Maps to router recipe 'triage-only'.

phases.name: adjudicate
phases.done_check: each finding marked real|false-positive|needs-evidence with cited live evidence; NO edits
phases.note: mh-rca confirms the real root cause read-only; mh-reviewer cross-checks the evidence in the report phase. Neither edits code.

phases.name: report
phases.done_check: triage verdict written (docs/audit/<date>/<scope>-triage.md); counts blob returned

batch-bugfix.summary: A raw LIST of bugs/findings -> drive to fixed. MIRRORS recipes.feature's distiller pattern: the PM is BLIND -- it NEVER reads the input list / findings / source. analyze (analyst reads+adjudicates+dedupes, tags EACH confirmed bug complexity->ROLE per tier_policy.roles) -> fix (fan-out ONE worker per confirmed bug/cluster via oc_worker by role / mh-implementer for clinical floor, gate-by-risk + harness gate re-validates every fix) -> final-review (MANDATORY Opus 4.8 xhigh review of the fix diff -- ALWAYS, even after cheap-model self-review; non-skippable owner invariant) -> downstream (OPTIONAL E2E hand-off). Maps to router recipe 'batch-bugfix'.
batch-bugfix.reference: main-brain/knowledge.md 'Batch Bugs By Dependency Phase' ; recipes.feature (distiller pattern, reused) ; engine/rules/context-manifest.md

phases.name: analyze
phases.done_check: adjudicated md written (docs/audit/<date>/<scope>-adjudicated.md): real-vs-false-positive decided + deduped by shared root cause + EACH confirmed bug tagged complexity->tier (complex->kimi-k2.7-code | medium->deepseek-v4-pro or minimax-m3 | simple/mechanical->deepseek-v4-flash | doc/test/chore->mimo-v2.5 | clinical/billing/permission/migration->opus-xhigh (floor) | whole-module/dangerous->opus-max) + risk_class; SUMMARY blob + adjudicated-md path returned
phases.note: MIRRORS recipes.feature 'distill': the PM does NOT read the input bug/findings list -- it dispatches the analyst (mh-rca, or a bug-analyst worker) which reads the raw list + source EXACTLY ONCE, adjudicates real vs false-positive, dedupes by shared root cause, WRITES one clean adjudicated md (the REAL bugs to fix), and tags EACH confirmed bug with a complexity->tier (complex->kimi-k2.7-code | medium->deepseek-v4-pro or minimax-m3 | simple/mechanical->deepseek-v4-flash | doc/test/chore->mimo-v2.5 | clinical/billing/permission/migration->opus-xhigh (floor) | whole-module/dangerous->opus-max) keyed off scripts/tier_policy.json -> roles + agentic_ladder (NOT the old anthropic tiers), plus risk_class. Returns ONLY a compact SUMMARY blob + the adjudicated-md path. The raw-list path lives in THIS worker's context-manifest only and is NEVER added to any later worker's manifest.

phases.name: fix
phases.fan_out: ONE worker PER CONFIRMED BUG / disjoint cluster, depth-1 from the MAIN LOOP, ordered BE -> contract/DTO-regen -> FE per 'Batch Bugs By Dependency Phase'. Non-Anthropic role -> a Claude shim runs `python scripts/oc_worker.py --role <bug.role> --scope <worktree> --allow-edit --prompt-file <bug-manifest> --out <artifact>`; clinical_floor/danger_refactor -> mh-implementer (Claude) at opus xhigh/max.
phases.tier: PER-BUG = scripts/tier_policy.json -> roles[bug.complexity_role] via agentic_ladder (simple/mechanical->deepseek-v4-flash | medium->deepseek-v4-pro/minimax-m3 | complex->kimi-k2.7-code | doc/test->mimo-v2.5), with risk_class_floor -> clinical_floor (opus xhigh) when bug.risk_class in {clinical,billing,permission,migration}, and danger_refactor (opus max) for whole-module/irreversible
phases.gate: computed-by-gate_by_risk PER BUG; EVERY fix (whatever model wrote it) is re-validated by the harness gate (tsc/dotnet build/scanner) before accept; gate-fail twice -> escalate up agentic_ladder
phases.done_check: every confirmed bug fixed in its worktree; per-bug harness-gate validation passed; 0 unresolved blockers
phases.note: BY-REFERENCE: each worker's context-manifest carries ONLY its bug -- the adjudicated-md row(s) for that one bug + rule_card(fe|be). Per-bug model is the analyst-assigned complexity ROLE (tier_policy.roles), NOT a whole-prompt risk_tier; clinical/billing/permission/migration -> opus xhigh floor. Cheap OpenCode models implement to spare Claude quota but are UNTRUSTED -> the harness gate + the mandatory Opus-xhigh self-review (next phase) are the safety net. MUST NOT dispatch mh-implement (depth-2) NOR incremental-impl.

phases.name: final-review
phases.dispatch: deep-review (+ mandatory Opus xhigh diff review)
phases.done_check: 0 BLOCK/HIGH open across the batched fix diff AND an Opus 4.8 xhigh review of the diff has run
phases.note: MANDATORY (owner invariant 2026-06-26, tier_policy.mandatory_final_review): every code-mutating batch MUST end with an Opus 4.8 xhigh review of the produced diff -- ALWAYS, even though cheap OpenCode models implemented and may have self-reviewed. Non-Anthropic review is ADDITIVE, never a substitute. FRESH-CONTEXT INDEPENDENT review (deep-review, whose surviving BLOCK/HIGH adjudicate at opus xhigh, or a dedicated opus-xhigh diff reviewer) -- NOT the implementer self-congratulating. Non-skippable.

phases.name: downstream
phases.done_check: OPTIONAL -- only when the workflow calls for it (e.g. hand off to a tester / robust-test for E2E repro of the fixed bugs); SKIP entirely otherwise
phases.note: OPTIONAL, workflow-dependent downstream hand-off (e.g. notify a tester or robust-test for E2E). Owner-gated workflow -> owner-confirm per gate_by_risk. Omit the phase when no downstream step is needed.

design-only.summary: The FRONT of `feature` WITHOUT implement: distill the BA doc (gated) -> spec (ui-spec | technical-design, gated) -> STOP. Produces 02-requirements + 03-ui/08-api for owner approval; no slicing, no source edits. Maps to router recipe 'design-only'.

phases.name: distill
phases.done_check: specs/<m>/02-requirements.md written + per-slice risk index (risk_tier+risk_class)
phases.note: Same distiller worker as recipes.feature: reads the BA doc EXACTLY ONCE -> 02-requirements + risk index. Hard owner-confirm gate.

phases.name: spec
phases.done_check: specs/<m>/03-ui.md and/or 08-api.md drafted + owner-approved
phases.note: From distilled 02-requirements + owner-authored 07-schema only (NOT the BA doc). Owner-gated SDD workflows -> always owner-confirm.

phases.name: STOP
phases.done_check: run ends after spec; NO slice/implement phase (design-only stops before code)
phases.note: Terminal marker: design-only deliberately omits slice+implement+converge. To build, the owner re-runs with the `feature` recipe.

gate_by_risk.auto_dispatch_when: phase is READ-ONLY (any tier -- read-only cannot damage) OR (risk_tier in {T0,T1} AND reversible-worktree-bounded). [Refined 2026-06-25 by the eval floor: a read-only review classified T3 by the router must still auto-dispatch; the tier cutoff applies to SOURCE-EDITING phases only.]
gate_by_risk.owner_confirm_when: a SOURCE-EDITING phase where risk_tier in {T2,T3} AND risk_class in {clinical,billing,permission,migration,irreversible-data}, OR phase.dispatch in {technical-design, task-slicing, ui-spec, dev-from-handoff, robust-test}
gate_by_risk.mid_run_business_ambiguity: espresso provisional-or-park protocol (PROVISIONAL-FIXED with cited basis + DL-PROV log, OR PARKED-NEEDS-OWNER for irreversible-data/migration) -- never silent invention, never mid-run blocking ask outside a gate

roles.coordinator: thin/blind; NO md read, NO rule load, NO source read/edit; per tick re-reads the ledger, checks the next phase gate, dispatches ONE worker from the MAIN LOOP, records the compact blob + artifact path, decides done. Writes only its own 00-pm-state.md ledger + 99-final-report.md (from blobs) and the .claude/.pm-run-active marker. Transfers memory to workers BY REFERENCE via a context-manifest. tier=coordinator
roles.worker: the dispatched unit (deep-review / mh-rca / mh-fix / mh-implement / espresso-test / a scribe agent). Self-hydrates from the context-manifest (rule_card + recommended_prompt_file + recalled notes + ledger path), does its bounded work, returns ONLY a compact blob (counts/paths/flags). tier=from-policy by risk_tier

artifact_contract.run_marker: .claude/.pm-run-active (contents = ledger path; created at run start, removed at run end)
